themes.title: Low-Dimensional Inorganic Synthesis & Exfoliation
themes.scientific_overview: This theme focuses on the design, solid-state or hydrothermal synthesis, and soft-chemical liquid exfoliation of layered and quasi-one-dimensional crystals. By exfoliating bulk crystals into single-layer nanosheets or single-chain nanoribbons, we explore quantum confinement and structural features at the atomic limit.
themes.why_it_matters: Exfoliated low-dimensional crystals serve as fundamental building blocks for constructing artificial heterostructures, advanced optical coatings, and nanoscale electronic devices that are otherwise impossible to fabricate through conventional thin-film deposition.
themes.current_challenges: Key challenges include achieving high-yield, defect-free exfoliation without chemical degradation and precisely sorting exfoliated layers by thickness and length.

themes.title: Charge-Density-Wave Devices & Phase Transitions
themes.scientific_overview: We investigate collective electronic phenomena, such as charge-density-wave (CDW) states, in quasi-one-dimensional Weyl semimetals (e.g., TaSe3, (TaSe4)2I) and quasi-two-dimensional transition-metal dichalcogenides (e.g., 1T-TaS2). This includes fabricating vertical and planar devices to control CDW phase sliding electrically at room temperature.
themes.why_it_matters: CDW phase-transition devices exhibit abrupt resistance switches that can operate at gigahertz frequencies. They are prime candidates for low-power microelectronics, radiation-immune electronics, and room-temperature oscillatory logic networks.
themes.current_challenges: Mitigating low-frequency electronic noise and stabilizing CDW phase coherence in devices smaller than 10 nanometers.

impact.discoveries: Pioneered the soft-chemical exfoliation of the ancient silicate pigment Egyptian blue into stable, single-layer near-infrared luminescent nanosheets.
impact.societal: Paving the way for ultra-low-power computing paradigms that address the exponential carbon footprint of modern AI data centers.
impact.industrial: Collaborating with national laboratories and microelectronics developers to prototype Weyl-semimetal-based interconnects that resist electromigration.
impact.future: Synthesizing novel mixed-dimensional superlattices and exploring topological edge states for room-temperature topological quantum computing.

timeline.title: Nanoscience of Egyptian Blue
timeline.description: Reported the first soft-chemical exfoliation of CaCuSi4O10 into monolayer silicate nanosheets, demonstrating NIR luminescence.

timeline.title: Room-Temperature CDW Oscillator
timeline.description: Co-developed an integrated 1T-TaS2/h-BN/graphene device demonstrating stable CDW current oscillations at room temperature.

timeline.title: Quasi-1D van der Waals Materials Review
timeline.description: Published a definitive overview in Materials Today establishing quasi-1D nanowires as a major new quantum materials research field.

timeline.title: 1D Atomic Chain Limit
timeline.description: Demonstrated the physical limit of liquid exfoliation, isolating individual atomic chains of MoI3 and Ta2Se8I.

timeline.title: In Situ STEM Dynamics
timeline.description: Observed real-time thermally-driven phase transformations of TaSe3 nanoribbons using in situ heating electron microscopy.
